question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger EVM calls against the staking reward account (0.0.800) while traceProperties.isEnabled() is true and getStatus() is empty (causing stateFilterCheck to always return false), causing full EVM frame data including pending reward amounts to be logged and observable, leading to theft of unpaid staking rewards by timing reward collection?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the condition in contractFilterCheck() where getContract().isEmpty() returns true (no contract filter set), causing every EVM contract execution to be traced and logged including staking reward precompile calls, leaking pending_reward values and stake_total_start for victim accounts, enabling targeted theft of unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract call that executes in a specific MessageFrame.State not present in the status set, bypassing stateFilterCheck() and causing the tracer to log the full input/output data of a staking reward calculation call, revealing the exact pending reward amount owed to a victim account, enabling front-running of reward collection?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call targeting the staking reward account address (0.0.800) when the contract set in TraceProperties is non-empty but does not include the staking reward contract address, causing contractFilterCheck() to return true and suppress tracing, while simultaneously using the absence of trace output as an oracle to confirm whether a staking reward precompile call succeeded, enabling silent theft of unpaid rewards?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that stateFilterCheck() returns false when getStatus() is empty (no filter), and use this knowledge to craft repeated EVM calls that force the tracer to emit logs containing the remainingGas and output data of staking reward balance queries, inferring the exact pending_reward balance of any account without authorization?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contractFilterCheck() uses a case-sensitive hex string comparison against the contract set, and submit a contract address with mixed-case hex encoding that bypasses the filter, causing the staking reward contract execution to be traced and its pending reward output data to be leaked in logs?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a contract call that transitions through MessageFrame.State.CODE_EXECUTING while tracing is enabled and the status set contains only CODE_SUSPENDED, causing stateFilterCheck() to return true and skip logging for the reward calculation frame, while a subsequent call in CODE_SUSPENDED state leaks the output data containing the computed pending reward amount?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user enumerate all staking-related contract addresses by submitting calls and observing which ones are suppressed by contractFilterCheck() (non-empty contract set, address not in set), using the presence or absence of trace log output as a side-channel to map the staking reward infrastructure and identify accounts with large pending_reward balances?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the logical inversion in stateFilterCheck() — which returns true (filter out) only when status is non-empty AND state is absent — by submitting calls that produce frames in every possible MessageFrame.State, ensuring at least one state passes the filter and logs the staking reward contract's input/output data, revealing pending reward amounts?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HashSet-based contract filter in TraceProperties where contractFilterCheck() performs a contains() lookup, and submit a contract address that collides with the staking reward account's EVM address representation, causing the filter to incorrectly pass or suppress the trace, leaking or hiding reward-related execution data?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user observe trace log output from stateFilterCheck() passing frames in CODE_SUSPENDED state that include the full hex-encoded output data of a BALANCE opcode call against the staking reward account 0.0.800, inferring the reserved_staking_rewards balance and timing a reward claim to maximize unpaid reward theft?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract that calls the staking reward precompile in a loop, and when contractFilterCheck() passes (empty contract filter), the tracer logs each iteration's output data including incremental pending_reward values, allowing the attacker to reconstruct the exact reward accumulation rate and predict the optimal time to trigger reward collection for maximum theft?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that stateFilterCheck() does not filter when getStatus() is empty, and submit a high-volume of eth_call requests targeting staking reward contracts, causing the tracer to emit logs that aggregate pending reward data across multiple accounts, enabling the attacker to identify the highest-value unpaid reward targets for theft?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Hex validation on the contract set in TraceProperties to understand the exact address format expected, and craft a contract call using a zero-padded EVM address for the staking reward account that bypasses contractFilterCheck() when the filter is non-empty, causing the reward contract execution to be traced and its output data (pending reward amount) to be logged?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract call that deliberately causes a REVERT in a specific MessageFrame.State that passes stateFilterCheck(), causing the tracer to log the revertReason field which may contain encoded pending reward data from a failed staking reward claim, leaking the victim's unpaid reward amount?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contractFilterCheck() compares the recipient entity's EVM address (derived from entity ID via toEvmAddress()) against the contract set, and submit a call where the recipient address resolves to a different entity ID than expected, bypassing the filter and causing staking reward contract traces to be logged with full input/output data?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between stateFilterCheck() and the BALANCE opcode tracking in MirrorOperationActionTracer, where a BALANCE call against the staking reward account sets balanceCall=true in ContractCallContext, and the subsequent trace log (when stateFilterCheck passes) reveals the exact balance of account 0.0.800, enabling calculation of available unpaid staking rewards?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the empty-set short-circuit in contractFilterCheck() — where an empty contract set means no filtering occurs — by submitting eth_call requests to the mirror node's web3 API that invoke staking reward precompile functions, causing the tracer to log the full ABI-encoded output including pending_reward values for arbitrary accounts?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that stateFilterCheck() returns false (allow tracing) when the status set is empty, and use the web3 eth_call endpoint to simulate a staking reward collection transaction, causing the tracer to log the output data containing the pending_reward amount that would be paid, enabling the attacker to verify reward amounts before submitting the actual transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HashSet<String> contract filter in TraceProperties by submitting a contract address that is a valid @Hex string of ADDRESS_LENGTH but represents a different entity than the staking reward account, causing contractFilterCheck() to incorrectly pass the staking reward contract and log its execution details including pending reward output data?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between stateFilterCheck() and the stake_period_start calculation, by submitting contract calls at specific times (near epoch day boundaries) when the EVM frame state transitions cause stateFilterCheck() to pass, logging the pending_reward computation output and enabling the attacker to claim rewards at the exact optimal moment?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contractFilterCheck() resolves the recipient address via commonEntityAccessor.get(recipientAddress, Optional.empty()), and submit a call with a recipient address that maps to the staking reward account entity ID, causing the filter to either pass or suppress the trace in a predictable way that leaks whether the staking reward account is being accessed?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of rate limiting on trace log generation when stateFilterCheck() passes all frames (empty status set), and flood the web3 API with contract calls targeting staking reward contracts, causing the system to log massive amounts of reward-related execution data that can be harvested to map all accounts with pending rewards?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @NonNull annotation on the contract set in TraceProperties combined with the empty-set behavior of contractFilterCheck(), by observing that when no contract filter is configured the tracer logs ALL contract executions, and use this to monitor staking reward precompile calls made by other users, stealing their pending reward timing information?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the stateFilterCheck() logic where !getStatus().isEmpty() && !getStatus().contains(state) returns false when the state IS in the set, and craft a contract call that forces the EVM frame into a state that is in the configured status set, ensuring the trace is logged and the staking reward contract's output data (pending_reward) is captured?
